summary: McKenzie recommends several newer micro-ISV (uISV) blogs to his readers, noting he tends to link only to established names. He highlights an A/B testing optimization blog, a tracker following the 'Class of 2007' micro-ISVs, and Planet MicroISV as an aggregator. Along the way, he offers a brief but pointed observation about the diminishing returns of optimizing for vanity metrics rather than revenue.
key_points: The micro-ISV blogging community was growing with newer voices worth following in 2008
A/B testing and conversion optimization were central preoccupations of the uISV community
Optimizing for metrics that don't translate to dollars or customer interest hits diminishing returns quickly
Community knowledge-sharing through blogs was a key growth mechanism for solo software entrepreneurs
money_quotes: There is a point after which optimizing for metrics not measured in dollars or other objective measurements of customer interest ceases to be productive.
I don't link out to uISV bloggers often enough and when I do it is often to the usual suspects.
Feel free to drop a link to your own blog, unless it is PartyPoker-HighestPayouts.co.ru.
key_insight: Optimizing for metrics that don't directly measure dollars or genuine customer interest eventually becomes unproductive, no matter how satisfying the charts look.
